seo.title: 20 Best Things To Do in Ayutthaya (2026 Guide)
seo.metaDescription: Discover the 20 best things to do in Ayutthaya in 2026 — from UNESCO temple ruins and the famous Buddha head in tree roots to cycling tours, roti sai mai tasting and sunset river cruises. Prices and tips included.

intro: Ayutthaya's appeal is overwhelmingly historical — this was the capital of Siam for over four centuries, and the temple ruins that remain are among the most atmospheric in Southeast Asia. But beyond the UNESCO-listed temples, you can cycle through quiet lanes, sample Ayutthaya's famous roti sai mai candy floss, cruise the river at sunset and explore a summer palace that mixes Thai, Chinese and Gothic architecture. Here are 20 things to do that cover culture, food, nature and tours — with real prices and practical tips.

activities.name: See the Buddha Head in Tree Roots at Wat Mahathat
activities.description: The most iconic image in Ayutthaya: a sandstone Buddha head perfectly framed by the gnarled roots of an ancient banyan tree. Nobody knows exactly how the head came to rest here — likely knocked from its body during the Burmese destruction of 1767, then slowly embraced by the growing roots over centuries. A single quiet moment here, crouched at eye level with the serene face, is worth the entire trip.
activities.price: 50 THB (~1.40 USD) or 220 THB combo ticket
tips: You must crouch or kneel when photographing the Buddha head — your head cannot be higher than the image
Arrive at 08:00 opening for the best light and fewest people
Explore the rest of the Wat Mahathat ruins — rows of headless Buddhas are equally striking

activities.name: Explore Wat Phra Si Sanphet's Three Royal Chedis
activities.description: The three bell-shaped chedis of Wat Phra Si Sanphet are Ayutthaya's most recognisable skyline — the equivalent of Bangkok's Grand Palace. Built between 1448 and 1530, they once contained the ashes of three Ayutthayan kings. Walk among the ruins of the former royal temple and imagine the grandeur of a city that once rivalled London and Paris in size.
activities.price: 50 THB (~1.40 USD) or 220 THB combo ticket
tips: Late afternoon light (15:00–17:00) makes the chedis glow
Walk next door to Viharn Phra Mongkhon Bophit (free) for one of Thailand's largest bronze Buddhas

activities.name: Watch Sunset at Wat Chaiwatthanaram
activities.description: Ayutthaya's most photogenic moment: the Khmer-style prang and eight surrounding chapels of Wat Chaiwatthanaram glowing orange against a darkening sky, reflected in the Chao Phraya River. Built in 1630 and inspired by Angkor Wat, this riverside temple is at its absolute best in the golden hour before closing time.
activities.price: 50 THB (~1.40 USD) or 220 THB combo ticket
tips: Arrive by 17:00 to secure a good viewpoint before sunset
Located outside the island — budget 15 minutes by bicycle from the centre
The temple is illuminated at night and visible from evening boat cruises

activities.name: Descend into the Crypt of Wat Ratchaburana
activities.description: While most Ayutthaya temples are ruins above ground, Wat Ratchaburana lets you descend steep stairs into a crypt beneath the main prang. Inside, you will find faded 15th-century frescoes — among the oldest surviving paintings in Thailand. Gold treasures discovered here in 1957 are now displayed at the nearby Chao Sam Phraya National Museum.
activities.price: 50 THB (~1.40 USD) or 220 THB combo ticket
tips: The descent is narrow and steep — wear sturdy shoes and avoid if claustrophobic
Visit the Chao Sam Phraya Museum afterwards to see the gold artefacts found here
Located directly opposite Wat Mahathat — visit both together

activities.name: Cycle Around the Island
activities.description: The best way to experience Ayutthaya is on two wheels. The historical island is flat, compact (roughly 4 km x 2 km) and crisscrossed by quiet roads shaded by old trees. Rent a bicycle from guesthouses near the train station and pedal between temples at your own pace, stopping for coconut water and temple cats along the way.
activities.price: 50–100 THB (~1.40–2.80 USD) bicycle rental per day
tips: Rent from shops on Naresuan Road or near the train station
Bring sunscreen, water and a hat — shade is limited between temples
A comfortable circuit covers 15–20 km visiting 6–8 temples
Watch for traffic at main road crossings — Thai drivers don't always yield

activities.name: Visit Wat Yai Chai Mongkhon
activities.description: This active temple southeast of the island is dominated by a massive 72-metre chedi built by King Naresuan the Great to celebrate his legendary single-combat victory over the Burmese crown prince on elephant-back in 1592. The grounds are lined with rows of Buddha statues draped in saffron cloth, and a large reclining Buddha rests peacefully in the gardens.
activities.price: 20 THB (~0.55 USD)
tips: Climb the chedi for panoramic views over the flat Ayutthaya landscape
Active temple — monks reside here, so be respectful and quiet
The rows of saffron-draped Buddhas are most photogenic in morning light

activities.name: Try Roti Sai Mai on Bang Ian Road
activities.description: Ayutthaya's signature street food: impossibly thin, candy-coloured cotton candy threads wrapped inside a paper-thin roti pancake. Watch vendors pull the sugar strands by hand on Bang Ian Road, where dozens of shops compete for the best roti sai mai in the city. The combination of warm, slightly salty roti with sweet, melt-in-your-mouth candy floss is addictive.
activities.price: 10–30 THB (~0.30–0.85 USD) per portion
tips: Bang Ian Road near Chao Phrom Market has the highest concentration of roti sai mai vendors
Buy a bag of assorted colours to take home as souvenirs — they keep for a few days
Watch the hand-pulling technique — it's mesmerising
activities.bestTime: Any time — vendors operate from morning to evening

sources.sourceName: Hong Thaimee — Day Trip to Ayutthaya

activities.name: Take a Sunset River Cruise
activities.description: Board a long-tail boat or converted rice barge for a sunset cruise along the Chao Phraya River. As the light fades, the temples along the riverbanks are illuminated, their reflections shimmering in the water. Most cruises pass Wat Chaiwatthanaram, Wat Phutthai Sawan and the old fortification walls.
activities.price: 200–500 THB (~5.60–14 USD) per person for group tours; 1,000–1,500 THB for private long-tail boat
tips: Book at guesthouses or at the pier near the Night Market
Dinner cruises (400–800 THB) include Thai food on board
Bring a light jacket — it can be cool on the river in the evening during cool season

activities.name: Visit Bang Pa-In Royal Palace
activities.description: This eclectic summer palace 20 km south of Ayutthaya blends Thai, Chinese, Gothic and Italian Renaissance architectural styles across manicured grounds. The Chinese-style Wehat Chamrun Palace, the Thai Aisawan Dhiphya-Asana pavilion reflected in the lake, and the Gothic Wat Niwet Thammaprawat (a Buddhist temple that looks like a European church) are the highlights.
tips: Strict dress code — long trousers/skirts and covered shoulders
Golf carts available for rent (400 THB) to cover the large grounds
Cross the river by cable car to visit Wat Niwet Thammaprawat — a Buddhist temple built like a European Gothic church
Can be combined with Ayutthaya on the way back to Bangkok

activities.name: Explore Chao Sam Phraya National Museum
activities.description: Ayutthaya's main museum provides essential context for the ruins. Highlights include gold regalia and jewellery found in the crypt of Wat Ratchaburana, carved door panels, ceramic trade goods from China and Japan, and a collection of bronze Buddha heads showing the evolution of Ayutthayan art. The air-conditioned galleries offer a welcome midday escape from the heat.
tips: Visit before the temples to understand the historical context
No photography inside the main exhibition halls
Open Wednesday–Sunday 09:00–16:00 (closed Mon–Tue)

activities.name: See the Giant Reclining Buddha at Wat Lokayasutharam
activities.description: An enormous 42-metre reclining Buddha lies in an open field surrounded by scattered ruins and ancient trees. Unlike the gilded indoor Reclining Buddha at Bangkok's Wat Pho, this weathered brick-and-stucco figure rests outdoors in a peaceful, park-like setting. The sheer scale — especially the feet — is impressive, and the lack of entrance barriers makes this an atmospheric, unhurried visit.
activities.price: Free
tips: No entrance gate — accessible at any time of day
Morning light is best (the Buddha faces east)
Combine with the western temples on your cycling route

activities.name: Visit Wat Phu Khao Thong (Golden Mount)
activities.description: Rising from the flat rice paddies 2 km northwest of the island, the 80-metre white chedi of Wat Phu Khao Thong is visible for kilometres around. Originally built by the Burmese during their brief occupation in 1569, it was later rebuilt in Ayutthayan style. Climb to the top for 360-degree views over the countryside — one of the best panoramas in the area, with almost no other tourists.
activities.price: Free
tips: The climb is manageable but steep — bring water
Almost no tourists — a peaceful contrast to the main temples
Best combined with a cycling trip through the rice paddies north of the island
activities.bestTime: Late afternoon for golden light on the white chedi

activities.name: Visit Ayutthaya Floating Market
activities.description: A modern, purpose-built floating market near the Ayutthaya Historical Park where vendors sell food, souvenirs and handicrafts from wooden stalls along a canal. While tourist-oriented rather than authentic, it is a pleasant place to sample local snacks, take a boat ride (20 THB) and browse affordable souvenirs in a shaded, canal-side setting.
activities.price: 200 THB (~5.60 USD) entrance; boat rides 20 THB; food 30–80 THB per dish
tips: It is more of a tourist market than a traditional floating market
Good place to try roti sai mai, boat noodles and grilled seafood
Open daily 09:00–18:00, busiest on weekends
Elephant shows were formerly held here but have been phased out

activities.name: Eat Boat Noodles (Kuay Teow Rua)
activities.description: Ayutthaya is considered the birthplace of boat noodles — intensely flavoured noodle soup traditionally sold from boats on the river. The bowls are small (often just a few bites), rich and dark with a pork or beef broth thickened with a dash of blood. Order 3–5 bowls for a full meal and stack the empties on your table — the more you stack, the more you have eaten.
activities.price: 10–15 THB (~0.30–0.40 USD) per small bowl; 3–5 bowls for a meal
tips: Try the boat noodle restaurants near the Floating Market or along Rojana Road
Order both pork (moo) and beef (neua) versions to compare
The small portion size is traditional — it is not a rip-off, just how it is served

sources.sourceName: Hong Thaimee — Day Trip to Ayutthaya

activities.name: Visit Wat Panang Choeng
activities.description: This active riverside temple predates the founding of Ayutthaya by 26 years — it was built in 1324. Inside the main viharn sits a colossal 19-metre gilded Buddha known as Luang Pho To, one of the most revered Buddha images in Thailand. The temple is popular with Thai and Thai-Chinese worshippers, and the atmosphere of active devotion is palpable.
activities.price: 20 THB (~0.55 USD)
tips: Located at the confluence of the Chao Phraya and Pa Sak rivers — accessible by boat or road
An active and popular worship site — visit with respect
The Chinese shrine next door is also worth seeing

activities.name: Explore Wat Phra Ram
activities.description: Set around a scenic reflection pond filled with lotus flowers, Wat Phra Ram was built in 1369 by King Ramesuan on the site where his father, King U Thong, was cremated. The central corn-cob-shaped prang rises from the water's edge, creating perfect reflections in the still water. A peaceful and uncrowded spot that most day-trippers miss.
activities.price: 50 THB (~1.40 USD) or included in 220 THB combo ticket
tips: The reflection pond makes for stunning photos, especially in morning light
Often uncrowded — a good contrast to busy Wat Mahathat
Walking distance from Wat Phra Si Sanphet
activities.bestTime: Morning for reflections in the pond

activities.name: Take the Train from Bangkok
activities.description: The train journey from Bangkok to Ayutthaya is a destination experience in itself. Board a 3rd-class carriage at Hua Lamphong station for 15–20 THB, sit on wooden benches with open windows, and watch the city give way to suburbs, then rice paddies, then the ancient towers of Ayutthaya emerging on the horizon. It is the most atmospheric and cheapest way to arrive.
activities.price: 15–345 THB (~0.40–10 USD) depending on class
tips: 3rd class is unreserved — arrive 15 minutes early for a window seat
Buy tickets at the station on the day — no advance booking needed for 3rd class
The Ayutthaya train station is a short ferry ride (5 THB) from the historical island
Trains run roughly hourly from early morning
activities.bestTime: Depart Bangkok 07:00–08:00 for a full day in Ayutthaya

activities.name: Sample Giant River Prawns (Kung Mae Nam)
activities.description: The rivers around Ayutthaya are famous for giant freshwater prawns — grilled whole over charcoal and served with a spicy seafood sauce. Head to the riverside restaurants on the east bank of the Pa Sak River, especially around the Hua Raw Night Market area, for some of the best and freshest river prawns in central Thailand.
activities.price: 200–500 THB (~5.60–14 USD) per portion depending on size
tips: The larger the prawn, the higher the price — ask before ordering
Riverside restaurants east of the island have the freshest supply
Best paired with som tum (papaya salad) and sticky rice

sources.sourceName: Hong Thaimee — Day Trip to Ayutthaya

activities.name: Visit the Ayutthaya Night Market
activities.description: The Chao Phrom Night Market, set up along the river near Chao Phrom Market, comes alive in the evening with food stalls, clothing vendors and live music. Thai families stroll, eat and socialise — the atmosphere is local and relaxed. This is the best place in Ayutthaya for an evening meal and a taste of everyday Thai life outside the tourist circuit.
activities.price: 40–100 THB (~1.10–2.80 USD) per dish
tips: Opens from 17:00 daily, busiest on weekends
Try the grilled meats, som tum, and fresh fruit shakes
Located near Chao Phrom Market in the centre of the island

sources.sourceName: Expats Thailand — Ayutthaya Day Trip Guide

activities.name: Explore Wat Thammikarat
activities.description: Known for the lion sculptures guarding its grounds and the atmospheric ruined chedi surrounded by old trees, Wat Thammikarat is one of the oldest temples in Ayutthaya — possibly predating the founding of the city. The main chedi is partially collapsed, creating dramatic ruins overgrown with vegetation. A large colony of roosters roams freely among the ruins.
activities.price: Free
tips: Free entry — one of the better free temples in the park
The lion sculptures at the entrance are unusual for Thai temples
The resident roosters add unexpected character
Quiet and uncrowded — good for atmospheric photos

sources.sourceName: Hong Thaimee — A Thai Chef's Guide to Ayutthaya
